# Sunnyside Decks & Fences — Complete Information

> Family-owned deck and fence builder based in Everett, Washington. Founded by Tim Lapatsin, Sunnyside Decks & Fences specializes in custom decks, premium fencing, pergolas, and complete outdoor living spaces designed for the Pacific Northwest climate. Owner Tim is on-site for every project — no subcontractor chains, no rotating crews.

## Entity Details

## About the Company

Sunnyside Decks & Fences — formerly operating as Lapatsin, LLC — is a family-run deck and fence building company rooted in Everett, Washington. Founded by Tim Lapatsin, the company was built on a straightforward idea: treat every client's backyard like your own. Tim brings years of hands-on carpentry and construction experience to every project, and he is personally on-site for every build. His crew specializes in custom decks, premium cedar fencing, pergolas, covered patios, and full outdoor living transformations designed to hold up against Pacific Northwest weather. The name change to Sunnyside Decks & Fences reflects the company's growth and its commitment to creating outdoor spaces where families actually want to spend time — rain or shine.

## Services (Full Descriptions)

### Custom Deck Building
Fully custom residential deck design and construction — single-level, multi-level, wraparound, and rooftop builds tailored to your home and lot. Tim handles design, framing, decking, railing, and finish work in-house, with materials and techniques chosen for Pacific Northwest conditions.

### Composite Decking
Trex, TimberTech, and PVC composite deck installation. Composite is the top low-maintenance choice for the PNW — fade-resistant, mold-resistant, and built to handle constant rain without rotting or warping. We install full composite systems including matching fascia, trim, and hidden fasteners.

### Wood Decking
Cedar, redwood, ipe hardwood, and pressure-treated lumber decks. For homeowners who want natural wood character, we recommend ipe (the longest-lasting option) or Western red cedar (a mid-range option with natural rot resistance). Every wood deck is built with stainless or hot-dipped galvanized hardware to handle PNW moisture.

### Deck Railings
Cable, aluminum, glass, vinyl, and wood railing systems. Cable railing is the most popular choice for waterfront and view properties because it preserves sightlines. All railings are installed code-compliant for Washington State and Snohomish County requirements.

### Covered Decks & Pergolas
Pergolas, covered patios, gazebos, and screened-in decks. Covered structures extend the usable outdoor season — critical in the Puget Sound where rain runs nine months out of the year. We build everything from simple shade pergolas to fully covered, lit, and heated outdoor rooms.

### Wood Fence Installation
Cedar privacy, horizontal slat, board-on-board, picket, and shadowbox cedar fencing. We use Western red cedar for the longest service life and build with proper post setting, drainage, and hardware to handle wet ground without sagging or rotting.

### Gate Installation
Driveway gates, garden gates, privacy gates, and fully custom gate design. We engineer gates to match the surrounding fence with hardware sized for the weight of the gate — preventing the sag-and-stick problem that wrecks most off-the-shelf installs.

### Outdoor Living Spaces
Multi-level decks, integrated deck lighting, fire features, and complete backyard design-build. For homeowners who want a full outdoor retreat, we coordinate decking, fencing, pergolas, lighting, and landscaping into one cohesive build.

## Service Area Cities

### Snohomish County
Everett (home base), Marysville, Lake Stevens, Arlington, Monroe, Snohomish, Mukilteo, Edmonds, Lynnwood, Mountlake Terrace, Mill Creek, Bothell

### King County
Seattle (north), Shoreline, Kenmore, Kirkland, Redmond, Woodinville, Bothell, Sammamish, Issaquah, Bellevue

### Skagit County
Mount Vernon, Burlington, Sedro-Woolley, Anacortes, La Conner, Stanwood

### Island County
Oak Harbor, Whidbey Island, Camano Island

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How much does a new deck cost in Everett, WA?
A: Deck costs in the Everett area typically range from $15,000 to $45,000+ depending on size, materials, and complexity. Composite decking like Trex runs higher than pressure-treated lumber but requires far less maintenance over time. We provide free on-site estimates with transparent, itemized pricing.

Q: What type of decking material is best for the Pacific Northwest?
A: Composite and hardwood (ipe) are the top choices for PNW weather — they resist moisture, rot, and mold better than standard pressure-treated wood. Cedar is a solid mid-range option with natural weather resistance. We walk you through the pros and cons of each during your consultation.

Q: Do I need a permit to build a deck or fence in Snohomish County?
A: Most deck projects over 30 inches above grade require a building permit in Snohomish County. Fence permits depend on height and location. Sunnyside handles the full permitting process for you so nothing holds up your project.

Q: How long does it take to build a deck?
A: Most residential decks take 1 to 3 weeks from start to finish, depending on size and complexity. Multi-level builds or projects requiring permits may take longer. We provide a clear timeline before work begins and keep you updated throughout.

Q: Do you offer free estimates?
A: Yes — every project starts with a free on-site consultation where Tim evaluates your space, discusses your vision, and provides a detailed estimate with no obligation.

Q: What sets Sunnyside Decks & Fences apart from larger contractors?
A: Tim is on-site daily for every project. There is no chain of subcontractors and no quote-and-disappear sales model. Every estimate is itemized in plain language, every material is something Tim would put on his own house, and every framing decision accounts for Pacific Northwest rain, freeze cycles, and moss pressure.

Q: Is Sunnyside Decks & Fences licensed and insured?
A: Yes. Sunnyside Decks & Fences is a fully licensed and insured deck and fence contractor based in Everett, Washington.

Q: What's the best fence material for the Pacific Northwest?
A: Western red cedar is our top recommendation for PNW fencing — it has natural rot and insect resistance, ages beautifully, and holds up to constant moisture better than pine or spruce. Properly installed cedar fencing lasts 20+ years.

Q: Do you handle the permitting process?
A: Yes. Tim handles permitting in-house for Snohomish County and the surrounding municipalities we serve. You don't need to navigate the paperwork yourself.

Q: Can you work with HOAs?
A: Yes. We have experience submitting plans for HOA architectural review across the Puget Sound and can provide the documentation HOAs typically require.
